title: Larger and more instructable language models become less reliable
data_sources: Zenodo repository (model responses and human study data)

data_scope.data_time_period: Models accessed 2022-2023; benchmark instances generated for this study

core_conclusion: Scaling up and instruction-tuning (shaping up) LLMs improves accuracy and prompt stability but paradoxically reduces reliability: shaped-up models eliminate avoidant responses and replace them with plausible-sounding incorrect answers (ultracrepidarianism), fail to consolidate safe operating zones at low difficulty, and generate errors that human supervisors frequently cannot detect. A fundamental shift toward predictable error distributions — not maximal accuracy — is required for high-stakes AI deployment.
why_it_matters: First systematic study showing that scaling up and shaping up simultaneously increase incorrectness by eliminating the avoidance safety buffer; introduces ultracrepidarianism as a measurable systemic risk invisible to accuracy-only benchmarks; demonstrates that human supervision cannot compensate; covers 32 models across GPT, LLaMA, and BLOOM families to disentangle scaling from shaping effects.
why_nature: Counter-intuitive paradigm-shifting result challenging the dominant scaling-law narrative; broad societal relevance across medicine, education, and administration; rigorous multi-study empirical design (~4.2M responses, two human studies); novel conceptual contribution (three-valued framework and six reliability indicators) as reusable measurement infrastructure.
added_value_to_my_research: Semantic entropy as live confidence gate for LLM-extracted spatial data (addresses, POI attributes) feeding into 2SFCA pipelines
Difficulty-weighted loss for fine-tuning geospatial foundation models — penalizing confident errors in data-sparse urban fringe areas
Reject-option LLM design for healthcare text parsing — generating explicit 'uncertain' flags for low-confidence spatial entity extractions
Prudence indicator as quality gate on LLM-extracted healthcare facility data, preventing hallucinated records from corrupting R2SFCA accessibility scores
Difficulty-aware training loop (meta-LLM assesses spatial prediction difficulty) aligned with spatially-aware loss functions in GeoAI ensembles

related.title: Detecting hallucinations in large language models using semantic entropy
related.relation: online hallucination detection complementing the paper's post-hoc reliability audit

related.title: Evaluating large language models for accuracy incentivizes hallucinations
related.relation: accuracy-driven evaluation incentivizes the ultracrepidarian behavior this paper documents

related.title: Training large language models on narrow tasks can lead to broad misalignment
related.relation: narrow fine-tuning can exacerbate the reliability collapse this paper identifies

related.title: Foundation models in healthcare require rethinking reliability
related.relation: extends reliability concerns from general LLMs to high-stakes healthcare foundation models

related.title: Optimizing generative AI by backpropagating language model feedback
related.relation: provides differentiable feedback mechanism to operationalize the paper's difficulty-concordance training objective
